get.summary: GetRecentRuns returns compliance runs matching the optional cluster, standard, and time filters.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to runs for the specified cluster.

parameters.name: standardId
parameters.description: standard_id restricts results to runs for the specified compliance standard.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: since filters runs to those that started at or after this timestamp.

post.summary: TriggerRuns starts new compliance runs for the specified cluster/standard selection.
post.description:
The selection supports wildcard values ("*") to trigger runs across all clusters
or all standards. Returns the list of ComplianceRun objects that were started.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cluster or standard selection cannot be resolved.
Requires write access to the Compliance resource.

get.summary: GetRunStatuses returns the current status for specified run IDs or the latest runs.
get.description:
Set latest=true to retrieve the most recent run per cluster/standard pair without
specifying IDs. Setting both latest=true and run_ids returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.
IDs in the request that do not match any run are returned in invalid_run_ids.

Requires read access to the Compliance resource.

parameters.name: runIds
parameters.description: run_ids is the list of compliance run IDs to query. Mutually exclusive with latest.

parameters.name: latest
parameters.description:
latest when true returns the most recent run for each cluster/standard pair,
ignoring run_ids. Cannot be combined with run_ids.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

clusterId.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster this run was executed against.

standardId.description: standard_id identifies the compliance standard used for this run.

startTime.description: start_time is when the compliance run began execution.

finishTime.description: finish_time is when the compliance run completed; zero if still in progress.

state.description: state is the current lifecycle stage of this run.

errorMessage.description: error_message contains details when the run failed; empty on success.

v1ComplianceRun.description: ComplianceRun represents a single compliance scan execution for a cluster/standard pair.

clusterId.description: cluster_id is the ID of the cluster to target. Use "*" to target all clusters.

standardId.description: standard_id is the ID of the compliance standard to target. Use "*" to target all standards.

v1ComplianceRunSelection.description:
ComplianceRunSelection specifies a cluster/standard pair for compliance run targeting.
Use "*" as a wildcard to target all clusters or all standards.

v1ComplianceRunState.description:
State describes the lifecycle stage of a compliance run.

 - READY: READY indicates the run is scheduled and waiting to start.
 - STARTED: STARTED indicates the run has been dispatched to the sensor.
 - WAIT_FOR_DATA: WAIT_FOR_DATA indicates the run is waiting for compliance data from the cluster.
 - EVALUTING_CHECKS: EVALUTING_CHECKS indicates the run is actively evaluating compliance checks.
 - FINISHED: FINISHED indicates the run has completed (successfully or with errors).

invalidRunIds.description: invalid_run_ids contains IDs from the request that were not found.

selection.description: selection specifies which clusters and standards to run. Use "*" in either field for all.
